content.thinking: Let me analyze this issue. The problem is that blockquotes in lists don't respect indentation to determine their nesting level. Let me explore the codebase to understand the tokenization logic.

1→const noopTest = { exec: () => null } as unknown as RegExp;
     2→
     3→function edit(regex: string | RegExp, opt = '') {
     4→  let source = typeof regex === 'string' ? regex : regex.source;
     5→  const obj = {
     6→    replace: (name: string | RegExp, val: string | RegExp) => {
     7→      let valSource = typeof val === 'string' ? val : val.source;
     8→      valSource = valSource.replace(other.caret, '$1');
     9→      source = source.replace(name, valSource);
    10→      return obj;
    11→    },
    12→    getRegex: () => {
    13→      return new RegExp(source, opt);
    14→    },
    15→  };
    16→  return obj;
    17→}
    18→
    19→const supportsLookbehind = (() => {
    20→try {
    21→  // eslint-disable-next-line prefer-regex-literals
    22→  return !!new RegExp('(?<=1)(?<!1)');
    23→} catch {
    24→  // See browser support here:
    25→  // https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Regular_expressions/Lookbehind_assertion
    26→  return false;
    27→}
    28→})();
    29→
    30→export const other = {
    31→  codeRemoveIndent: /^(?: {1,4}| {0,3}\t)/gm,
    32→  outputLinkReplace: /\\([\[\]])/g,
    33→  indentCodeCompensation: /^(\s+)(?:```)/,
    34→  beginningSpace: /^\s+/,
    35→  endingHash: /#$/,
    36→  startingSpaceChar: /^ /,
    37→  endingSpaceChar: / $/,
    38→  nonSpaceChar: /[^ ]/,
    39→  newLineCharGlobal: /\n/g,
    40→  tabCharGlobal: /\t/g,
    41→  multipleSpaceGlobal: /\s+/g,
    42→  blankLine: /^[ \t]*$/,
    43→  doubleBlankLine: /\n[ \t]*\n[ \t]*$/,
    44→  blockquoteStart: /^ {0,3}>/,
    45→  blockquoteSetextReplace: /\n {0,3}((?:=+|-+) *)(?=\n|$)/g,
    46→  blockquoteSetextReplace2: /^ {0,3}>[ \t]?/gm,
    47→  listReplaceTabs: /^\t+/,
    48→  listReplaceNesting: /^ {1,4}(?=( {4})*[^ ])/g,
    49→  listIsTask: /^\[[ xX]\] +\S/,
    50→  listReplaceTask: /^\[[ xX]\] +/,
    51→  listTaskCheckbox: /\[[ xX]\]/,
    52→  anyLine: /\n.*\n/,
    53→  hrefBrackets: /^<(.*)>$/,
    54→  tableDelimiter: /[:|]/,
    55→  tableAlignChars: /^\||\| *$/g,
    56→  tableRowBlankLine: /\n[ \t]*$/,
    57→  tableAlignRight: /^ *-+: *$/,
    58→  tableAlignCenter: /^ *:-+: *$/,
    59→  tableAlignLeft: /^ *:-+ *$/,
    60→  startATag: /^<a /i,
    61→  endATag: /^<\/a>/i,
    62→  startPreScriptTag: /^<(pre|code|kbd|script)(\s|>)/i,
    63→  endPreScriptTag: /^<\/(pre|code|kbd|script)(\s|>)/i,
    64→  startAngleBracket: /^</,
    65→  endAngleBracket: />$/,
    66→  pedanticHrefTitle: /^([^'"]*[^\s])\s+(['"])(.*)\2/,
    67→  unicodeAlphaNumeric: /[\p{L}\p{N}]/u,
    68→  escapeTest: /[&<>"']/,
    69→  escapeReplace: /[&<>"']/g,
    70→  escapeTestNoEncode: /[<>"']|&(?!(#\d{1,7}|#[Xx][a-fA-F0-9]{1,6}|\w+);)/,
    71→  escapeReplaceNoEncode: /[<>"']|&(?!(#\d{1,7}|#[Xx][a-fA-F0-9]{1,6}|\w+);)/g,
    72→  unescapeTest: /&(#(?:\d+)|(?:#x[0-9A-Fa-f]+)|(?:\w+));?/ig,
    73→  caret: /(^|[^\[])\^/g,
    74→  percentDecode: /%25/g,
    75→  findPipe: /\|/g,
    76→  splitPipe: / \|/,
    77→  slashPipe: /\\\|/g,
    78→  carriageReturn: /\r\n|\r/g,
    79→  spaceLine: /^ +$/gm,
    80→  notSpaceStart: /^\S*/,
    81→  endingNewline: /\n$/,
    82→  listItemRegex: (bull: string) => new RegExp(`^( {0,3}${bull})((?:[\t ][^\\n]*)?(?:\\n|$))`),
    83→  nextBulletRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}(?:[*+-]|\\d{1,9}[.)])((?:[ \t][^\\n]*)?(?:\\n|$))`),
    84→  hrRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}((?:- *){3,}|(?:_ *){3,}|(?:\\* *){3,})(?:\\n+|$)`),
    85→  fencesBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}(?:\`\`\`|~~~)`),
    86→  headingBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}#`),
    87→  htmlBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}<(?:[a-z].*>|!--)`, 'i'),

    89→
    90→/**
    91→ * Block-Level Grammar
    92→ */
    93→
    94→const newline = /^(?:[ \t]*(?:\n|$))+/;
    95→const blockCode = /^((?: {4}| {0,3}\t)[^\n]+(?:\n(?:[ \t]*(?:\n|$))*)?)+/;
    96→const fences = /^ {0,3}(`{3,}(?=[^`\n]*(?:\n|$))|~{3,})([^\n]*)(?:\n|$)(?:|([\s\S]*?)(?:\n|$))(?: {0,3}\1[~`]* *(?=\n|$)|$)/;
    97→const hr = /^ {0,3}((?:-[\t ]*){3,}|(?:_[ \t]*){3,}|(?:\*[ \t]*){3,})(?:\n+|$)/;
    98→const heading = /^ {0,3}(#{1,6})(?=\s|$)(.*)(?:\n+|$)/;
    99→const bullet = /(?:[*+-]|\d{1,9}[.)])/;
   100→const lheadingCore = /^(?!bull |blockCode|fences|blockquote|heading|html|table)((?:.|\n(?!\s*?\n|bull |blockCode|fences|blockquote|heading|html|table))+?)\n {0,3}(=+|-+) *(?:\n+|$)/;
   101→const lheading = edit(lheadingCore)
   102→  .replace(/bull/g, bullet) // lists can interrupt
   103→  .replace(/blockCode/g, /(?: {4}| {0,3}\t)/) // indented code blocks can interrupt
   104→  .replace(/fences/g, / {0,3}(?:`{3,}|~{3,})/) // fenced code blocks can interrupt
   105→  .replace(/blockquote/g, / {0,3}>/) // blockquote can interrupt
   106→  .replace(/heading/g, / {0,3}#{1,6}/) // ATX heading can interrupt
   107→  .replace(/html/g, / {0,3}<[^\n>]+>\n/) // block html can interrupt
   108→  .replace(/\|table/g, '') // table not in commonmark
   109→  .getRegex();
   110→const lheadingGfm = edit(lheadingCore)
   111→  .replace(/bull/g, bullet) // lists can interrupt
   112→  .replace(/blockCode/g, /(?: {4}| {0,3}\t)/) // indented code blocks can interrupt
   113→  .replace(/fences/g, / {0,3}(?:`{3,}|~{3,})/) // fenced code blocks can interrupt
   114→  .replace(/blockquote/g, / {0,3}>/) // blockquote can interrupt
   115→  .replace(/heading/g, / {0,3}#{1,6}/) // ATX heading can interrupt
   116→  .replace(/html/g, / {0,3}<[^\n>]+>\n/) // block html can interrupt
   117→  .replace(/table/g, / {0,3}\|?(?:[:\- ]*\|)+[\:\- ]*\n/) // table can interrupt
   118→  .getRegex();
   119→const _paragraph = /^([^\n]+(?:\n(?!hr|heading|lheading|blockquote|fences|list|html|table| +\n)[^\n]+)*)/;
   120→const blockText = /^[^\n]+/;
   121→const _blockLabel = /(?!\s*\])(?:\\[\s\S]|[^\[\]\\])+/;
   122→const def = edit(/^ {0,3}\[(label)\]: *(?:\n[ \t]*)?([^<\s][^\s]*|<.*?>)(?:(?: +(?:\n[ \t]*)?| *\n[ \t]*)(title))? *(?:\n+|$)/)
   123→  .replace('label', _blockLabel)
   124→  .replace('title', /(?:"(?:\\"?|[^"\\])*"|'[^'\n]*(?:\n[^'\n]+)*\n?'|\([^()]*\))/)
   125→  .getRegex();
   126→
   127→const list = edit(/^( {0,3}bull)([ \t][^\n]+?)?(?:\n|$)/)
   128→  .replace(/bull/g, bullet)
   129→  .getRegex();
   130→
   131→const _tag = 'address|article|aside|base|basefont|blockquote|body|caption'
   132→  + '|center|col|colgroup|dd|details|dialog|dir|div|dl|dt|fieldset|figcaption'
   133→  + '|figure|footer|form|frame|frameset|h[1-6]|head|header|hr|html|iframe'
   134→  + '|legend|li|link|main|menu|menuitem|meta|nav|noframes|ol|optgroup|option'
   135→  + '|p|param|search|section|summary|table|tbody|td|tfoot|th|thead|title'
   136→  + '|tr|track|ul';
   137→const _comment = /<!--(?:-?>|[\s\S]*?(?:-->|$))/;
   138→const html = edit(
   139→  '^ {0,3}(?:' // optional indentation
   140→+ '<(script|pre|style|textarea)[\\s>][\\s\\S]*?(?:</\\1>[^\\n]*\\n+|$)' // (1)
   141→+ '|comment[^\\n]*(\\n+|$)' // (2)
   142→+ '|<\\?[\\s\\S]*?(?:\\?>\\n*|$)' // (3)
   143→+ '|<![A-Z][\\s\\S]*?(?:>\\n*|$)' // (4)
   144→+ '|<!\\[CDATA\\[[\\s\\S]*?(?:\\]\\]>\\n*|$)' // (5)
   145→+ '|</?(tag)(?: +|\\n|/?>)[\\s\\S]*?(?:(?:\\n[ \t]*)+\\n|$)' // (6)
   146→+ '|<(?!script|pre|style|textarea)([a-z][\\w-]*)(?:attribute)*? */?>(?=[ \\t]*(?:\\n|$))[\\s\\S]*?(?:(?:\\n[ \t]*)+\\n|$)' // (7) open tag
   147→+ '|</(?!script|pre|style|textarea)[a-z][\\w-]*\\s*>(?=[ \\t]*(?:\\n|$))[\\s\\S]*?(?:(?:\\n[ \t]*)+\\n|$)' // (7) closing tag
   148→+ ')', 'i')
   149→  .replace('comment', _comment)
   150→  .replace('tag', _tag)
   151→  .replace('attribute', / +[a-zA-Z:_][\w.:-]*(?: *= *"[^"\n]*"| *= *'[^'\n]*'| *= *[^\s"'=<>`]+)?/)
   152→  .getRegex();
   153→
   154→const paragraph = edit(_paragraph)
   155→  .replace('hr', hr)
   156→  .replace('heading', ' {0,3}#{1,6}(?:\\s|$)')
   157→  .replace('|lheading', '') // setext headings don't interrupt commonmark paragraphs
   158→  .replace('|table', '')
   159→  .replace('blockquote', ' {0,3}>')
   160→  .replace('fences', ' {0,3}(?:`{3,}(?=[^`\\n]*\\n)|~{3,})[^\\n]*\\n')
   161→  .replace('list', ' {0,3}(?:[*+-]|1[.)]) ') // only lists starting from 1 can interrupt
   162→  .replace('html', '</?(?:tag)(?: +|\\n|/?>)|<(?:script|pre|style|textarea|!--)')

   171→ * Normal Block Grammar
   172→ */
   173→
   174→const blockNormal = {
   175→  blockquote,
   176→  code: blockCode,

   178→  fences,
   179→  heading,
   180→  hr,
   181→  html,
   182→  lheading,
   183→  list,

   185→  paragraph,

   187→  text: blockText,

   189→
   190→type BlockKeys = keyof typeof blockNormal;

   192→/**
   193→ * GFM Block Grammar
   194→ */
   195→
   196→const gfmTable = edit(
   197→  '^ *([^\\n ].*)\\n' // Header
   198→+ ' {0,3}((?:\\| *)?:?-+:? *(?:\\| *:?-+:? *)*(?:\\| *)?)' // Align
   199→+ '(?:\\n((?:(?! *\\n|hr|heading|blockquote|code|fences|list|html).*(?:\\n|$))*)\\n*|$)') // Cells
   200→  .replace('hr', hr)
   201→  .replace('heading', ' {0,3}#{1,6}(?:\\s|$)')
   202→  .replace('blockquote', ' {0,3}>')
   203→  .replace('code', '(?: {4}| {0,3}\t)[^\\n]')
   204→  .replace('fences', ' {0,3}(?:`{3,}(?=[^`\\n]*\\n)|~{3,})[^\\n]*\\n')
   205→  .replace('list', ' {0,3}(?:[*+-]|1[.)]) ') // only lists starting from 1 can interrupt
   206→  .replace('html', '</?(?:tag)(?: +|\\n|/?>)|<(?:script|pre|style|textarea|!--)')
   207→  .replace('tag', _tag) // tables can be interrupted by type (6) html blocks
   208→  .getRegex();
   209→
   210→const blockGfm: Record<BlockKeys, RegExp> = {
   211→  ...blockNormal,
   212→  lheading: lheadingGfm,
   213→  table: gfmTable,
   214→  paragraph: edit(_paragraph)
   215→    .replace('hr', hr)
   216→    .replace('heading', ' {0,3}#{1,6}(?:\\s|$)')
   217→    .replace('|lheading', '') // setext headings don't interrupt commonmark paragraphs
   218→    .replace('table', gfmTable) // interrupt paragraphs with table
   219→    .replace('blockquote', ' {0,3}>')
   220→    .replace('fences', ' {0,3}(?:`{3,}(?=[^`\\n]*\\n)|~{3,})[^\\n]*\\n')
   221→    .replace('list', ' {0,3}(?:[*+-]|1[.)]) ') // only lists starting from 1 can interrupt
   222→    .replace('html', '</?(?:tag)(?: +|\\n|/?>)|<(?:script|pre|style|textarea|!--)')
   223→    .replace('tag', _tag) // pars can be interrupted by type (6) html blocks
   224→    .getRegex(),
   225→};
   226→
   227→/**
   228→ * Pedantic grammar (original John Gruber's loose markdown specification)
   229→ */
   230→
   231→const blockPedantic: Record<BlockKeys, RegExp> = {
   232→  ...blockNormal,
   233→  html: edit(
   234→    '^ *(?:comment *(?:\\n|\\s*$)'
   235→    + '|<(tag)[\\s\\S]+?</\\1> *(?:\\n{2,}|\\s*$)' // closed tag
   236→    + '|<tag(?:"[^"]*"|\'[^\']*\'|\\s[^\'"/>\\s]*)*?/?> *(?:\\n{2,}|\\s*$))')
   237→    .replace('comment', _comment)
   238→    .replace(/tag/g, '(?!(?:'
   239→      + 'a|em|strong|small|s|cite|q|dfn|abbr|data|time|code|var|samp|kbd|sub'
   240→      + '|sup|i|b|u|mark|ruby|rt|rp|bdi|bdo|span|br|wbr|ins|del|img)'
   241→      + '\\b)\\w+(?!:|[^\\w\\s@]*@)\\b')
   242→    .getRegex(),
   243→  def: /^ *\[([^\]]+)\]: *<?([^\s>]+)>?(?: +(["(][^\n]+[")]))? *(?:\n+|$)/,
   244→  heading: /^(#{1,6})(.*)(?:\n+|$)/,
   245→  fences: noopTest, // fences not supported
   246→  lheading: /^(.+?)\n {0,3}(=+|-+) *(?:\n+|$)/,
   247→  paragraph: edit(_paragraph)
   248→    .replace('hr', hr)
   249→    .replace('heading', ' *#{1,6} *[^\n]')
   250→    .replace('lheading', lheading)
   251→    .replace('|table', '')
   252→    .replace('blockquote', ' {0,3}>')
   253→    .replace('|fences', '')
   254→    .replace('|list', '')
   255→    .replace('|html', '')
   256→    .replace('|tag', '')
   257→    .getRegex(),

   259→
   260→/**
   261→ * Inline-Level Grammar
   262→ */
   263→
   264→const escape = /^\\([!"#$%&'()*+,\-./:;<=>?@\[\]\\^_`{|}~])/;
   265→const inlineCode = /^(`+)([^`]|[^`][\s\S]*?[^`])\1(?!`)/;
   266→const br = /^( {2,}|\\)\n(?!\s*$)/;
   267→const inlineText = /^(`+|[^`])(?:(?= {2,}\n)|[\s\S]*?(?:(?=[\\<!\[`*_]|\b_|$)|[^ ](?= {2,}\n)))/;
   268→
   269→// list of unicode punctuation marks, plus any missing characters from CommonMark spec
   270→const _punctuation = /[\p{P}\p{S}]/u;
   271→const _punctuationOrSpace = /[\s\p{P}\p{S}]/u;
   272→const _notPunctuationOrSpace = /[^\s\p{P}\p{S}]/u;
   273→const punctuation = edit(/^((?![*_])punctSpace)/, 'u')
   274→  .replace(/punctSpace/g, _punctuationOrSpace).getRegex();
   275→
   276→// GFM allows ~ inside strong and em for strikethrough
   277→const _punctuationGfmStrongEm = /(?!~)[\p{P}\p{S}]/u;
   278→const _punctuationOrSpaceGfmStrongEm = /(?!~)[\s\p{P}\p{S}]/u;
   279→const _notPunctuationOrSpaceGfmStrongEm = /(?:[^\s\p{P}\p{S}]|~)/u;
   280→
   281→// GFM allows * and _ inside strikethrough
   282→const _punctuationGfmDel = /(?![*_])[\p{P}\p{S}]/u;
   283→const _punctuationOrSpaceGfmDel = /(?![*_])[\s\p{P}\p{S}]/u;
   284→const _notPunctuationOrSpaceGfmDel = /(?:[^\s\p{P}\p{S}]|[*_])/u;
   285→
   286→// sequences em should skip over [title](link), `code`, <html>
   287→const blockSkip = edit(/link|precode-code|html/, 'g')
   288→  .replace('link', /\[(?:[^\[\]`]|(?<a>`+)[^`]+\k<a>(?!`))*?\]\((?:\\[\s\S]|[^\\\(\)]|\((?:\\[\s\S]|[^\\\(\)])*\))*\)/)
   289→  .replace('precode-', supportsLookbehind ? '(?<!`)()' : '(^^|[^`])')
   290→  .replace('code', /(?<b>`+)[^`]+\k<b>(?!`)/)
   291→  .replace('html', /<(?! )[^<>]*?>/)

   293→
   294→const emStrongLDelimCore = /^(?:\*+(?:((?!\*)punct)|[^\s*]))|^_+(?:((?!_)punct)|([^\s_]))/;
   295→
   296→const emStrongLDelim = edit(emStrongLDelimCore, 'u')
   297→  .replace(/punct/g, _punctuation)
   298→  .getRegex();
   299→
   300→const emStrongLDelimGfm = edit(emStrongLDelimCore, 'u')
   301→  .replace(/punct/g, _punctuationGfmStrongEm)
   302→  .getRegex();
   303→
   304→const emStrongRDelimAstCore =
   305→  '^[^_*]*?__[^_*]*?\\*[^_*]*?(?=__)' // Skip orphan inside strong
   306→+ '|[^*]+(?=[^*])' // Consume to delim
   307→+ '|(?!\\*)punct(\\*+)(?=[\\s]|$)' // (1) #*** can only be a Right Delimiter
   308→+ '|notPunctSpace(\\*+)(?!\\*)(?=punctSpace|$)' // (2) a***#, a*** can only be a Right Delimiter
   309→+ '|(?!\\*)punctSpace(\\*+)(?=notPunctSpace)' // (3) #***a, ***a can only be Left Delimiter
   310→+ '|[\\s](\\*+)(?!\\*)(?=punct)' // (4) ***# can only be Left Delimiter
   311→+ '|(?!\\*)punct(\\*+)(?!\\*)(?=punct)' // (5) #***# can be either Left or Right Delimiter
   312→+ '|notPunctSpace(\\*+)(?=notPunctSpace)'; // (6) a***a can be either Left or Right Delimiter
   313→
   314→const emStrongRDelimAst = edit(emStrongRDelimAstCore, 'gu')
   315→  .replace(/notPunctSpace/g, _notPunctuationOrSpace)
   316→  .replace(/punctSpace/g, _punctuationOrSpace)
   317→  .replace(/punct/g, _punctuation)
   318→  .getRegex();
   319→
   320→const emStrongRDelimAstGfm = edit(emStrongRDelimAstCore, 'gu')
   321→  .replace(/notPunctSpace/g, _notPunctuationOrSpaceGfmStrongEm)
   322→  .replace(/punctSpace/g, _punctuationOrSpaceGfmStrongEm)
   323→  .replace(/punct/g, _punctuationGfmStrongEm)
   324→  .getRegex();
   325→
   326→// (6) Not allowed for _
   327→const emStrongRDelimUnd = edit(
   328→  '^[^_*]*?\\*\\*[^_*]*?_[^_*]*?(?=\\*\\*)' // Skip orphan inside strong
   329→+ '|[^_]+(?=[^_])' // Consume to delim
   330→+ '|(?!_)punct(_+)(?=[\\s]|$)' // (1) #___ can only be a Right Delimiter
   331→+ '|notPunctSpace(_+)(?!_)(?=punctSpace|$)' // (2) a___#, a___ can only be a Right Delimiter
   332→+ '|(?!_)punctSpace(_+)(?=notPunctSpace)' // (3) #___a, ___a can only be Left Delimiter
   333→+ '|[\\s](_+)(?!_)(?=punct)' // (4) ___# can only be Left Delimiter
   334→+ '|(?!_)punct(_+)(?!_)(?=punct)', 'gu') // (5) #___# can be either Left or Right Delimiter
   335→  .replace(/notPunctSpace/g, _notPunctuationOrSpace)
   336→  .replace(/punctSpace/g, _punctuationOrSpace)
   337→  .replace(/punct/g, _punctuation)
   338→  .getRegex();
   339→
   340→// Tilde left delimiter for strikethrough (similar to emStrongLDelim for asterisk)
   341→const delLDelim = edit(/^~~?(?:((?!~)punct)|[^\s~])/, 'u')
   342→  .replace(/punct/g, _punctuationGfmDel)
   343→  .getRegex();
   344→
   345→// Tilde delimiter patterns for strikethrough (similar to asterisk)
   346→const delRDelimCore =
   347→  '^[^~]+(?=[^~])' // Consume to delim
   348→+ '|(?!~)punct(~~?)(?=[\\s]|$)' // (1) #~~ can only be a Right Delimiter
   349→+ '|notPunctSpace(~~?)(?!~)(?=punctSpace|$)' // (2) a~~#, a~~ can only be a Right Delimiter
   350→+ '|(?!~)punctSpace(~~?)(?=notPunctSpace)' // (3) #~~a, ~~a can only be Left Delimiter
   351→+ '|[\\s](~~?)(?!~)(?=punct)' // (4) ~~# can only be Left Delimiter
   352→+ '|(?!~)punct(~~?)(?!~)(?=punct)' // (5) #~~# can be either Left or Right Delimiter
   353→+ '|notPunctSpace(~~?)(?=notPunctSpace)'; // (6) a~~a can be either Left or Right Delimiter
   354→
   355→const delRDelim = edit(delRDelimCore, 'gu')
   356→  .replace(/notPunctSpace/g, _notPunctuationOrSpaceGfmDel)
   357→  .replace(/punctSpace/g, _punctuationOrSpaceGfmDel)
   358→  .replace(/punct/g, _punctuationGfmDel)
   359→  .getRegex();
   360→
   361→const anyPunctuation = edit(/\\(punct)/, 'gu')
   362→  .replace(/punct/g, _punctuation)
   363→  .getRegex();
   364→
   365→const autolink = edit(/^<(scheme:[^\s\x00-\x1f<>]*|email)>/)
   366→  .replace('scheme', /[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9+.-]{1,31}/)
   367→  .replace('email', /[a-zA-Z0-9.!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(@)[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?)+(?![-_])/)
   368→  .getRegex();
   369→
   370→const _inlineComment = edit(_comment).replace('(?:-->|$)', '-->').getRegex();
   371→const tag = edit(
   372→  '^comment'
   373→    + '|^</[a-zA-Z][\\w:-]*\\s*>' // self-closing tag
   374→    + '|^<[a-zA-Z][\\w-]*(?:attribute)*?\\s*/?>' // open tag
   375→    + '|^<\\?[\\s\\S]*?\\?>' // processing instruction, e.g. <?php ?>
   376→    + '|^<![a-zA-Z]+\\s[\\s\\S]*?>' // declaration, e.g. <!DOCTYPE html>
   377→    + '|^<!\\[CDATA\\[[\\s\\S]*?\\]\\]>') // CDATA section
   378→  .replace('comment', _inlineComment)
   379→  .replace('attribute', /\s+[a-zA-Z:_][\w.:-]*(?:\s*=\s*"[^"]*"|\s*=\s*'[^']*'|\s*=\s*[^\s"'=<>`]+)?/)
   380→  .getRegex();
   381→
   382→const _inlineLabel = /(?:\[(?:\\[\s\S]|[^\[\]\\])*\]|\\[\s\S]|`+[^`]*?`+(?!`)|[^\[\]\\`])*?/;
   383→
   384→const link = edit(/^!?\[(label)\]\(\s*(href)(?:(?:[ \t]*(?:\n[ \t]*)?)(title))?\s*\)/)
   385→  .replace('label', _inlineLabel)
   386→  .replace('href', /<(?:\\.|[^\n<>\\])+>|[^ \t\n\x00-\x1f]*/)
   387→  .replace('title', /"(?:\\"?|[^"\\])*"|'(?:\\'?|[^'\\])*'|\((?:\\\)?|[^)\\])*\)/)
   388→  .getRegex();
   389→
   390→const reflink = edit(/^!?\[(label)\]\[(ref)\]/)
   391→  .replace('label', _inlineLabel)
   392→  .replace('ref', _blockLabel)
   393→  .getRegex();
   394→
   395→const nolink = edit(/^!?\[(ref)\](?:\[\])?/)
   396→  .replace('ref', _blockLabel)
   397→  .getRegex();
   398→
   399→const reflinkSearch = edit('reflink|nolink(?!\\()', 'g')
   400→  .replace('reflink', reflink)
   401→  .replace('nolink', nolink)
   402→  .getRegex();
   403→
   404→const _caseInsensitiveProtocol = /[hH][tT][tT][pP][sS]?|[fF][tT][pP]/;
   405→
   406→/**
   407→ * Normal Inline Grammar
   408→ */
   409→
   410→const inlineNormal = {
   411→  _backpedal: noopTest, // only used for GFM url
   412→  anyPunctuation,
   413→  autolink,
   414→  blockSkip,
   415→  br,
   416→  code: inlineCode,
   417→  del: noopTest,
   418→  delLDelim: noopTest,
   419→  delRDelim: noopTest,
   420→  emStrongLDelim,
   421→  emStrongRDelimAst,
   422→  emStrongRDelimUnd,
   423→  escape,
   424→  link,
   425→  nolink,
   426→  punctuation,
   427→  reflink,
   428→  reflinkSearch,
   429→  tag,
   430→  text: inlineText,
   431→  url: noopTest,
   432→};
   433→
   434→type InlineKeys = keyof typeof inlineNormal;
   435→
   436→/**
   437→ * Pedantic Inline Grammar
   438→ */
   439→
   440→const inlinePedantic: Record<InlineKeys, RegExp> = {
   441→  ...inlineNormal,
   442→  link: edit(/^!?\[(label)\]\((.*?)\)/)
   443→    .replace('label', _inlineLabel)
   444→    .getRegex(),
   445→  reflink: edit(/^!?\[(label)\]\s*\[([^\]]*)\]/)
   446→    .replace('label', _inlineLabel)
   447→    .getRegex(),
   448→};
   449→
   450→/**
   451→ * GFM Inline Grammar
   452→ */
   453→
   454→const inlineGfm: Record<InlineKeys, RegExp> = {
   455→  ...inlineNormal,
   456→  emStrongRDelimAst: emStrongRDelimAstGfm,
   457→  emStrongLDelim: emStrongLDelimGfm,
   458→  delLDelim,
   459→  delRDelim,
   460→  url: edit(/^((?:protocol):\/\/|www\.)(?:[a-zA-Z0-9\-]+\.?)+[^\s<]*|^email/)
   461→    .replace('protocol', _caseInsensitiveProtocol)
   462→    .replace('email', /[A-Za-z0-9._+-]+(@)[a-zA-Z0-9-_]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9-_]*[a-zA-Z0-9])+(?![-_])/)
   463→    .getRegex(),
   464→  _backpedal: /(?:[^?!.,:;*_'"~()&]+|\([^)]*\)|&(?![a-zA-Z0-9]+;$)|[?!.,:;*_'"~)]+(?!$))+/,
   465→  del: /^(~~?)(?=[^\s~])((?:\\[\s\S]|[^\\])*?(?:\\[\s\S]|[^\s~\\]))\1(?=[^~]|$)/,
   466→  text: edit(/^([`~]+|[^`~])(?:(?= {2,}\n)|(?=[a-zA-Z0-9.!#$%&'*+\/=?_`{\|}~-]+@)|[\s\S]*?(?:(?=[\\<!\[`*~_]|\b_|protocol:\/\/|www\.|$)|[^ ](?= {2,}\n)|[^a-zA-Z0-9.!#$%&'*+\/=?_`{\|}~-](?=[a-zA-Z0-9.!#$%&'*+\/=?_`{\|}~-]+@)))/)
   467→    .replace('protocol', _caseInsensitiveProtocol)
   468→    .getRegex(),
   469→};
   470→
   471→/**
   472→ * GFM + Line Breaks Inline Grammar
   473→ */
   474→
   475→const inlineBreaks: Record<InlineKeys, RegExp> = {
   476→  ...inlineGfm,
   477→  br: edit(br).replace('{2,}', '*').getRegex(),
   478→  text: edit(inlineGfm.text)
   479→    .replace('\\b_', '\\b_| {2,}\\n')
   480→    .replace(/\{2,\}/g, '*')
   481→    .getRegex(),
   482→};
   483→
   484→/**
   485→ * exports
   486→ */
   487→
   488→export const block = {
   489→  normal: blockNormal,
   490→  gfm: blockGfm,
   491→  pedantic: blockPedantic,
   492→};
   493→
   494→export const inline = {
   495→  normal: inlineNormal,
   496→  gfm: inlineGfm,
   497→  breaks: inlineBreaks,
   498→  pedantic: inlinePedantic,
   499→};
   500→
   501→export interface Rules {
   502→  other: typeof other
   503→  block: Record<BlockKeys, RegExp>
   504→  inline: Record<InlineKeys, RegExp>
   505→}
   506→

In the else branch, the code checks whether to break out of the item parsing or continue accumulating content as a paragraph continuation—it breaks if it encounters a blank line, an indented code block, or any block-level element like a fence, heading, or horizontal rule.

findPipe: /\|/g,
  splitPipe: / \|/,
  slashPipe: /\\\|/g,
  carriageReturn: /\r\n|\r/g,
  spaceLine: /^ +$/gm,
  notSpaceStart: /^\S*/,
  endingNewline: /\n$/,
  listItemRegex: (bull: string) => new RegExp(`^( {0,3}${bull})((?:[\t ][^\\n]*)?(?:\\n|$))`),
  nextBulletRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}(?:[*+-]|\\d{1,9}[.)])((?:[ \t][^\\n]*)?(?:\\n|$))`),
  hrRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}((?:- *){3,}|(?:_ *){3,}|(?:\\* *){3,})(?:\\n+|$)`),
  fencesBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}(?:\`\`\`|~~~)`),
  headingBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}#`),
  htmlBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}<(?:[a-z].*>|!--)`, 'i'),
};

content.text:
All 158 tests pass, 0 failures. The fix is complete.

**Summary of changes:**

- **`src/rules.ts`**: Added `blockquoteBeginRegex` alongside the existing `fencesBeginRegex`, `headingBeginRegex`, and `htmlBeginRegex` helpers. It matches a `>` character preceded by at most `Math.min(3, indent - 1)` spaces — the same indentation pattern as the other break-condition regexes.

- **`src/Tokenizer.ts`**: In the list item's inner line-collection loop, added a break check using `blockquoteBeginRegex`. If a `>` appears with fewer spaces than the current list item's content indentation, it breaks out of that item — the same logic already used for headings, fences, and HTML blocks.

This makes blockquote indentation work like all other block-level elements: the number of leading spaces on the `>` determines which list item (if any) the blockquote belongs to.

result:
All 158 tests pass, 0 failures. The fix is complete.

**Summary of changes:**

- **`src/rules.ts`**: Added `blockquoteBeginRegex` alongside the existing `fencesBeginRegex`, `headingBeginRegex`, and `htmlBeginRegex` helpers. It matches a `>` character preceded by at most `Math.min(3, indent - 1)` spaces — the same indentation pattern as the other break-condition regexes.

- **`src/Tokenizer.ts`**: In the list item's inner line-collection loop, added a break check using `blockquoteBeginRegex`. If a `>` appears with fewer spaces than the current list item's content indentation, it breaks out of that item — the same logic already used for headings, fences, and HTML blocks.

This makes blockquote indentation work like all other block-level elements: the number of leading spaces on the `>` determines which list item (if any) the blockquote belongs to.
